query_normalized: predicting amine reactivity using graph neural networks and public databases chemistry
rationale: This paper qualifies under category (d) as a foundational methodology reference for Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and category (f) as a canonical study on graph topology (homophily/heterophily), which are the core structural mechanisms the user's proposed model relies on to predict chemical reactivity.
